proven.claim: Model: diff.isGitRepo() is represented as an integer (1 = true, 0 = false).
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1 - Precondition Propagation (prove that the error is only reachable when the directory is not a git repository)
; Model: diff.isGitRepo() is represented as an integer (1 = true, 0 = false).
; The code executes `console.error(...)` exactly when (!diff.isGitRepo()).
; We encode the transition and then assert the negation of the intended property:
;   errorReached ⇒ diff_is_git_repo = 0
; Negating gives: errorReached ∧ diff_is_git_repo = 1, which must be unsatisfiable.

(declare-const diff_is_git_repo Int)   ; 1 = repository, 0 = not a repository
(declare-const errorReached Int)       ; 1 = console.error executed, 0 = not executed

; Transition: the program sets errorReached to true iff diff_is_git_repo is false.
(assert (= errorReached (ite (= diff_is_git_repo 0) 1 0)))

; Negated property (the bug we want to prove impossible):
; error is executed while diff_is_git_repo is true.
(assert (= errorReached 1))
(assert (= diff_is_git_repo 1))

(check-sat)
; Expected result: unsat → the error cannot be reached when the directory *is* a git repo.

clause_history.clause:
; PRINCIPLE: P1 - Precondition Propagation (prove that the error is only reachable when the directory is not a git repository)
; Model: diff.isGitRepo() is represented as an integer (1 = true, 0 = false).
; The code executes `console.error(...)` exactly when (!diff.isGitRepo()).
; We encode the transition and then assert the negation of the intended property:
;   errorReached ⇒ diff_is_git_repo = 0
; Negating gives: errorReached ∧ diff_is_git_repo = 1, which must be unsatisfiable.

(declare-const diff_is_git_repo Int)   ; 1 = repository, 0 = not a repository
(declare-const errorReached Int)       ; 1 = console.error executed, 0 = not executed

; Transition: the program sets errorReached to true iff diff_is_git_repo is false.
(assert (= errorReached (ite (= diff_is_git_repo 0) 1 0)))

; Negated property (the bug we want to prove impossible):
; error is executed while diff_is_git_repo is true.
(assert (= errorReached 1))
(assert (= diff_is_git_repo 1))

(check-sat)
; Expected result: unsat → the error cannot be reached when the directory *is* a git repo.
